NCT01844986.name: SOLO-1
NCT01844986.group: BRCA+ high-grade ovarian cancer, 1st-line maintenance olaparib vs placebo
NCT01844986.snippet: Source: Moore K et al. NEJM 2018;379:2495-2505 (SOLO-1); 7-yr OS DiSilvestro et al. JCO 2023.

evidence.label: Enrollment & Randomization
evidence.source: Moore K et al. NEJM 2018;379:2495-2505, CONSORT
evidence.text: 391 patients with newly diagnosed FIGO III/IV high-grade serous/endometrioid ovarian cancer and germline or somatic BRCA1/2 mutation who had complete or partial response to platinum-based chemo were randomized 2:1 to olaparib 300 mg BID maintenance (n=260) or placebo (n=131) for up to 2 years.

evidence.label: Primary — Progression-Free Survival
evidence.source: Moore K et al. NEJM 2018, Figure 2
evidence.text: Median investigator-assessed PFS: not reached olaparib vs 13.8 months placebo. HR 0.30 (95% CI 0.23-0.41; P<0.001). 3-year PFS: 60% vs 27%.

evidence.label: 7-year OS Update (2023)
evidence.text: 7-year OS: 67.0% olaparib vs 46.5% placebo; HR 0.55 (95% CI 0.40-0.76; P=0.0004). First statistically significant OS benefit with 1st-line PARP maintenance.

evidence.label: Eligibility (AACT-derived)
evidence.text: AACT-derived (auto-populated 2026-05-04). Sex: FEMALE; min age 18 Years; max age 130 Years; healthy volunteers: f. CRITERIA: "Inclusion Criteria:~* Female patients with newly diagnosed, histologically confirmed, high risk advanced (FIGO stage III - IV) BRCA mutated high grade serous or high grade endometrioid ovarian cancer, primary peritoneal cancer and / or fallopian - tube cancer who have completed first line platinum based chemotherapy (intravenous or intraperitoneal).~* Stage III patients must have had one attempt at optimal debulking surgery (upfront or interval debulking). Stage IV patients must have had either...

evidence.label: Demographics (AACT-derived)
evidence.text: AACT-derived (auto-populated 2026-05-04). Race: Global Cohort 0, Global Cohort 1, Global Cohort 1, Global Cohort 39, Global Cohort 20. NOTE: figures are total-arms aggregate from CT.gov baseline-measurements module; per-arm breakdown available in AACT raw data. RACE caveat: review trial-population racial composition for external-validity considerations.

NCT02477644.name: PAOLA-1
NCT02477644.group: All-comer high-grade ovarian, 1st-line maintenance olaparib + bevacizumab vs placebo + bevacizumab
NCT02477644.snippet: Source: Ray-Coquard I et al. NEJM 2019;381:2416-2428 (PAOLA-1); final OS Ray-Coquard Ann Oncol 2023.

evidence.label: Enrollment & Randomization
evidence.source: Ray-Coquard I et al. NEJM 2019, CONSORT
evidence.text: 806 patients with newly diagnosed FIGO III/IV high-grade ovarian cancer in response to platinum + bevacizumab were randomized 2:1 to olaparib 300 mg BID + bevacizumab maintenance (n=537) vs placebo + bevacizumab maintenance (n=269). All-comer population (HRD+ and HRD- stratified).

evidence.label: Primary — Progression-Free Survival (ITT)
evidence.source: Ray-Coquard I et al. NEJM 2019, Figure 2
evidence.text: Median investigator-assessed PFS: 22.1 months olaparib+bev vs 16.6 months placebo+bev. HR 0.59 (95% CI 0.49-0.72; P<0.001). HRD-positive subgroup (n=387): median PFS 37.2 vs 17.7 months; HR 0.33.

evidence.label: Overall Survival Final (2023)
evidence.source: Ray-Coquard I et al. Ann Oncol 2023 (PAOLA-1 5yr)
evidence.text: Final ITT OS HR 1.00 (0.80-1.26); no OS benefit in unselected population. HRD+ subgroup: HR 0.62 (0.45-0.85) — significant OS benefit restricted to HRD positive.

evidence.label: Eligibility (AACT-derived)
evidence.text: AACT-derived (auto-populated 2026-05-04). Sex: FEMALE; min age 18 Years; healthy volunteers: f. CRITERIA: "Inclusion Criteria:~I-1. Female Patient must be ≥18 years of age. I-2. Signed informed consent and ability to comply with treatment and follow-up.~I-3. Patient with newly diagnosed I-3-1 Ovarian cancer, primary peritoneal cancer and/or fallopian-tube cancer,~I-3-2 Histologically confirmed (based on local histopathological findings):~* high grade serous or~* high grade endometrioid or~* other epithelial non mucinous ovarian cancer in a patient with germline BRCA 1 or 2 deleterious mutation I-3-3...

NCT02655016.name: PRIMA
NCT02655016.group: All-comer high-grade ovarian, 1st-line maintenance niraparib vs placebo
NCT02655016.snippet: Source: Gonzalez-Martin A et al. NEJM 2019;381:2391-2402 (PRIMA); final OS 2023 ESMO update.

evidence.label: Enrollment & Randomization
evidence.text: 733 patients with newly diagnosed FIGO III/IV high-grade serous/endometrioid ovarian cancer in complete/partial response to platinum chemotherapy were randomized 2:1 to niraparib 200-300 mg daily maintenance (n=487) vs placebo (n=246). All-comer population (HRD+ subgroup pre-specified).

evidence.label: Primary — Progression-Free Survival (ITT)
evidence.text: Median BICR PFS: 13.8 months niraparib vs 8.2 months placebo. HR 0.62 (95% CI 0.50-0.76; P<0.001). HRD+ subgroup: median 21.9 vs 10.4 months; HR 0.43 (0.31-0.59).

evidence.label: Overall Survival Final
evidence.text: Final OS HR 1.01 (0.84-1.23) in ITT; no OS benefit in unselected population. HRD+ subgroup numerically favorable but not statistically significant.

evidence.label: Eligibility (AACT-derived)
evidence.text: AACT-derived (auto-populated 2026-05-04). Sex: FEMALE; min age 18 Years; healthy volunteers: f. CRITERIA: "Inclusion criteria:~* Participants must have histologically diagnosed high-grade serous or endometrioid, or high-grade predominantly serous or endometrioid ovarian cancer, fallopian tube cancer, or primary peritoneal cancer that is Stage III or IV according to Federation Internationale de Gynécologie et d'Obstétrique (FIGO) criteria.~* Participants with inoperable Stage III and IV disease; All Stage IV participants with operable disease; Participants with stage III or IV disease treated with ne...

title: RapidMeta Oncology | PARP Inhibitor Maintenance in First-Line Ovarian Cancer v1.0
